title: Proposal and Quote Follow-Up for E-Commerce
metaDescription: Done-for-you proposal and quote follow-up for e-commerce. Your AIOS chases open quotes on a schedule, in your voice, until they close or get a clear no.
excerpt: Stop letting quotes for wholesale, custom, or bulk orders go cold. Your AIOS follows up on every open proposal in your voice, on a steady cadence.

sections.intro:
Most online retailers are tuned for self-serve checkout, but a real chunk of revenue still moves through quotes. Wholesale orders, bulk pricing for B2B buyers, custom bundles, branded packaging, dropship partners, and big-ticket items all need a proposal or a quote, and then they need chasing. That chasing is where money quietly leaks. The owner sends the quote, the buyer goes quiet, and a week later it is buried under shipping issues, supplier emails, and new orders.

The AIOS fixes the leak. It watches every proposal and quote you send, then runs a steady follow-up cadence in your voice using live data from your inbox, CRM, and billing tools. No quote sits unanswered. No buyer slips through because you were busy fulfilling orders. You stay in control, the system just stops letting work fall on the floor.
sections.fourCFit:
Proposal and quote follow-up lives in the Capabilities layer of your AIOS. Capabilities are the done-for-you workflows that draft, route, summarize, follow up, and report on your behalf. This one sits next to the other three layers:

  • Context: the system knows your product range, your typical quote terms, your buyer types, and your voice.
  • Connections: it reads from your inbox, CRM, quoting or billing tool, and calendar so it works from live data, not guesses.
  • Capabilities: this workflow, drafting and sending follow-ups, logging replies, surfacing hot quotes.
  • Cadence: it runs on a schedule, day 2, day 5, day 10, day 21, without you asking.
sections.whatDoneLooksLike:
When this capability is installed, here is the artifact you get and the service level it runs at:

  • Every quote or proposal you send is logged automatically with buyer, value, products, and date.
  • Follow-up emails go out on a fixed cadence (typical default: day 2 nudge, day 5 value reminder, day 10 check-in, day 21 final call) in your written voice.
  • Replies are routed back to you with a one-line summary and a suggested next step.
  • Quotes that go cold after the final touch are closed-lost with a reason, not left open forever.
  • A weekly digest shows open quote value, win rate, and which buyers need a human nudge.
  • SLA: no open quote goes more than 48 hours past its scheduled follow-up without action.
sections.howWeInstall:
Installation is a three-phase process. You do not build anything. We do.

  1. Audit (free, about 10 minutes): we look at your current quoting flow, how many quotes you send per month, your average close window, and where follow-ups currently die. Run the audit.
  2. Install (10 business days): we connect your inbox, CRM, and quoting tool, capture your voice from past emails, build the follow-up cadence, and test it on real open quotes before going live.
  3. Operate (ongoing): the workflow runs on schedule, we monitor it, and we add one new automation to your AIOS every week. See plans.
sections.expectedResults:
Real ranges we see when this capability is installed for e-commerce operators with regular wholesale or custom quote volume:

  • Quote close rate up 15 to 35 percent, mostly from quotes that would have gone cold.
  • Average time to close down 20 to 40 percent because buyers get the nudge while the order is still top of mind.
  • Owner time spent on follow-up emails down to near zero, replaced by 10 to 15 minutes a week reviewing the digest.
  • Clean pipeline data, so you actually know what is open, what is dead, and what is worth chasing personally.
  • Standing guarantee: your AIOS recovers 40 or more hours a month across the system, or we keep working free until it does.
sections.faq:
Will buyers know the follow-ups are not written by me in the moment?

No. The system writes in your voice using your past emails as the reference, and you can approve templates before they go live. The cadence and tone match how you already write to wholesale and B2B buyers.

What if a buyer replies with a real question or objection?

The reply is routed straight to you with a one-line summary and the original quote context. The system never tries to negotiate or quote on your behalf. It hands the human conversation back to you the moment it starts.

Do I need to switch quoting tools or CRMs?

No. The AIOS is tool-agnostic and connects to what you already use for quotes, your inbox, and your CRM. If your current stack is doing the job, we work with it.
